meta.description: Matches a manuscript abstract to target journals using Tier 1/2/3 classification, NLP/clinical-trial/methodology-aware scoring, mandatory IF disclaimer, and open-access filter. Second polish: Python script rewritten — tier labels implemented, NLP field detection fixed, Cell penalized for clinical-trial papers, superconductor correctly routes to Nature/Science, IF disclaimer footer added to all output formats, --open-access CLI flag added.

scientific_integrity.detail: Impact factor values sourced from bundled journals.json; no fabricated IFs, DOIs, PMIDs, or clinical outcome data.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic or prescriptive medical conclusions produced; skill is limited to journal recommendations.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies; no ethical compliance requirements triggered by journal-matching task.

code_usability.detail: Script (main.py) runs successfully on Python 3.9. Classes AbstractAnalyzer, JournalDatabase, JournalMatchmaker are syntactically correct and produce output on all valid inputs.

functional_suitability.note: Tier 1/2/3 classification implemented in script output (all formats). NLP/CV field disambiguation fixed. Methodology penalty for clinical-trial vs basic-science journals. Multidisciplinary paradigm-shift detection added. --open-access filter added.

reliability.note: Fault Tolerance (3/4): short-abstract validation works; auto-creates default database if journals.json missing. Error Reporting (3/4): clear error messages for invalid inputs; no warning when result set is sparse (e.g., only 2 journals found for environmental topics). Recoverability (3/4): stateless CLI; each run is independent.

performance_context.note: Token Cost (3/4): script output is concise. Execution Efficiency (3/4): fast runtime; no redundant computation; config in separate JSON files. Minor: no caching for repeated abstract analysis.

agent_usability.note: Mandatory IF disclaimer now embedded in all output formats. Tier-grouped output improves feedback design. Study design printed to console before recommendations.

human_usability.note: Discoverability (3/4): markdown output is clean and readable. Forgiveness (3/4): no destructive operations; errors are informative.

security.note: Full marks. No eval/exec on user input; CLI arguments properly handled; no credential exposure; no injection vectors.

maintainability.note: Score-to-tier mapping is a standalone function (easy to recalibrate). BASIC_SCIENCE_ONLY_JOURNALS set is externally updatable. Separate methodology penalty function.

agent_specific.note: Tier labels enable progressive disclosure (Tier 1→2→3 decision tree). --open-access escape hatch added. Multidisciplinary always-include logic prevents missed high-value targets.

inputs.label: CRISPR gene editing in stem cells (biology/medicine)
inputs.note: Tier 1/2/3 labels now present in output (Tier 1: Cell, Nat Med, Nat Methods, Nat Biotechnol; Tier 1: Cell Res). IF disclaimer present. Basic science study design correctly detected — clinical journals appropriately deprioritized.

key_strengths: Structured CLI interface with multiple output formats (table, json, markdown) and configurable --min-if / --max-if filtering enables realistic tier-scoped searches
Self-creating default journal database (auto-generates journals.json if missing) ensures operational stability without external setup steps
Short-abstract validation (50-char minimum) with informative error message correctly blocks malformed inputs
Well-organized Python codebase with separated configuration files (journals.json, fields.json, scoring_weights.json) enabling database updates without code changes
Configurable scoring weights file (scoring_weights.json) allows tunable matching behavior without rewriting logic

recommendations.title: Tier 1/2/3 classification described in SKILL.md is absent from script output
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md describes a four-step workflow whose Step 4 (Deliver) explicitly mentions Tier 1 (ambition), Tier 2 (realistic fit), and Tier 3 (safe target) recommendations. No tier labels appear in any script output across all 5 test inputs.
recommendations.root_cause: The JournalMatchmaker class returns a flat ranked list sorted by relevance score without tier segmentation logic.
recommendations.fix: Add tier classification to JournalMatchmaker.recommend(): apply SKILL.md scoring thresholds (e.g., score >= 0.65 = Tier 1, 0.45–0.65 = Tier 2, < 0.45 = Tier 3) and label each recommendation accordingly in output.

recommendations.title: Field detection errors cause irrelevant journal recommendations
recommendations.problem: Input 2 (NLP paper): computer vision journals (TPAMI, IJCV) ranked above NLP-specific journals (TACL, Computational Linguistics) present in journals.json. Input 5 (superconductor paper): field 'chemistry' incorrectly triggers Environmental Science & Technology; Nature/Science (multidisciplinary) omitted entirely.
recommendations.root_cause: FIELD_KEYWORDS dict in AbstractAnalyzer uses simple keyword overlap without subfield hierarchy; 'chemistry' maps to environmental_science journals; multidisciplinary journals are not boosted for paradigm-shift language.
recommendations.fix: Refine FIELD_KEYWORDS to weight NLP-specific terms toward nlp/computational_linguistics fields. Add a 'scope_text_match' component that searches abstract text against journal scope strings for fine-grained differentiation.

recommendations.title: IF accuracy disclaimer required by SKILL.md hard rule absent from all script outputs
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md hard rule states: 'Always note that IF is approximate and should be verified.' Zero outputs contain this disclaimer despite it being a hard rule in the skill definition.
recommendations.root_cause: Script output templates (table, json, markdown) have no footer/caveat section for hard-rule disclaimers.
recommendations.fix: Add a fixed footer to all three output formats: 'Note: Impact factors are approximate and based on database snapshot as of [journals.json date]. Verify current IF at journal websites before submission. Journal acceptance is not guaranteed.'

recommendations.title: Methodology-to-scope matching absent — scoring dominated by IF and field tag
recommendations.problem: Input 4: Cell (basic biology, IF 64.5) ranks equal to Nature Medicine for a large 1,240-patient RCT; NEJM/Lancet/JAMA — the canonical RCT venues — rank below. Scoring does not account for study type (RCT vs. basic science vs. computational).
recommendations.root_cause: method_match component (weight 0.20) is not implemented in the scoring logic despite being listed in scoring_weights.json; match_reasons output shows only 'Field alignment' and 'High impact factor'.
recommendations.fix: Implement method_match scoring: detect study design keywords (RCT, clinical trial, cohort, computational, simulation, in vitro) from abstract and cross-reference against journal scope descriptions. Boost clinical trial journals for RCT abstracts.

recommendations.title: Journal database skews toward top-tier (31 journals, IF > 4 majority) leaving most fields without mid-range options
recommendations.problem: Input 3 (minimal environmental abstract) returns only 2 journals. Input 5 returns only 4. Most researchers needing Tier 2/3 recommendations cannot find mid-range options in the current database.
recommendations.root_cause: Default journal database (_create_default_database) contains 31 manually curated high-profile journals. No procedure for expanding coverage.
recommendations.fix: Expand journals.json to include 20–30 additional mid-range journals (IF 2–10) per major field, or document a clear procedure for users to add journals to journals.json.

recommendations.title: No open-access-only filter despite SKILL.md mentioning OA as a practical consideration
recommendations.problem: journals.json includes an 'open_access' boolean field, but no CLI flag exposes OA filtering to users. Researchers with OA mandates have no way to restrict results.
recommendations.root_cause: JournalMatchmaker.recommend() does not expose the open_access field as a filter parameter.
recommendations.fix: Add --open-access flag to CLI: when set, filter recommendations to journals where open_access=true. Add corresponding note in SKILL.md.
